name: Energy Sector (Global)
summary: The primary beneficiary sector of the crisis. XLE (Energy ETF) +13.6% monthly, +40.2% quarterly. Exxon +15.1% monthly. Cheniere (LNG) +27.7% monthly. While every other sector sells off, energy stocks rally on higher oil/gas prices. This is the 'war winner' rotation — capital flows from tech/consumer into energy. Indian equivalents: Reliance, ONGC, Oil India benefit from higher realizations.

signals.content: XLE +1.7% on the day while S&P -1.7% and Nifty -2.1%. The divergence is widening — energy is the only sector gaining during the crisis. Exxon +3.4% ($170.99), Cheniere +1.9% ($296.91, +54.6% quarterly). Defense sector (ITA) diverging — now -11% monthly despite war, due to Pentagon budget uncertainty. The rotation is oil/gas, not defense. (Sources: yfinance)

edges.context: Energy company revenues directly tied to oil/gas prices. Higher Brent = higher earnings = higher stock prices. Inverse of every other sector.
Capital rotating from tech/consumer into energy. XLE gaining while SPX falls. If crisis persists, energy sector weight in S&P increases.

trigger_points.condition: Energy sector becomes largest S&P 500 sector by weight
trigger_points.mechanism: Would signal a structural regime shift in the US equity market — from tech-led to energy-led. Last happened briefly in 2008 and 2022.
